problem: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared?
mechanism: Photodynamic therapy targeting specific cell receptors
hypothesis: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Photodynamic. Photodynamic therapy targeting specific cell receptors directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Retrospective cohort analysis with 30 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.
